title: Rented Properties Information About a House

groups.title: Rented Properties Information About a House
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Available date: $1$ Prices: • rent: $2$ $ per month • deposit: $1,500 • $3$: $15 Facilities: • 3 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms • a remodelled $4$ • no $5$ • a $6$ with a work area Utilities: • The landlord will provide landscaping service, but the tenants must $7$ the grass. • The tenants should pay $15 for trashing and $8$ service. • The tenants should pay for electricity, water and gas bills. Other information: There is no central air conditioning, but there is a $9$ conditioning unit. Name of landlord: Sam $10$

questions.title: The tenants should pay $15 for trashing and 【   】 service.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Hello?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Oh, hello.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: I wanted to enquire about the house you're going to let out.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Is it still available?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: May I ask where you heard about it?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Was it in an advert?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Right, it was.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: OK, what do you want to know?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Well, I'm going to share the house with two friends.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: We've been staying on campus, but we're going to graduate, so we need to find a house as soon as possible.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Anyway, could you tell me the exact date when we can move in?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Well, the contract with my current tenant is due next week, on May 3rd.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: And I need a day to clean and check the things in the house, so I think the house will be ready on 5th May.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Is that OK?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Yes, that will be fine.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: And what we're most concerned about is the rent.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: We'd like something around 500 dollars a month for each of us, but we could go up to 600 dollars if we have to.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: But we can't go beyond that.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: You are lucky – the rent will be 1,700 dollars in total, which is within your budget.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: You'll need to pay monthly to me, by cash or credit card, I don't mind.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: And you'll have to pay me 1,500 dollars as a deposit before you move in.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: The deposit is as insurance, in case you break something.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: OK, that's reasonable.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Oh, I nearly forgot.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: One important thing is that you'll be required to provide a credit check to guarantee your ability to pay the rent every month.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: This will charge you 15 dollars.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: No problem.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: How about the facilities?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: First there are three bedrooms combined with two bathrooms.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Then there is a shared kitchen which was refurbished several months ago.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: By the way, do you cook yourselves?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Yeah, we like cooking.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Do we have to buy our own cookers?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Basic facilities for cooking are available, but if you want a dishwasher, I'm sorry it needs to be added later.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: I think the basic facilities are enough for us.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Is there any place we can park?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: As one of my friends has a private car.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Yes, there is a garage which not only provides space for your car but also has a special section where you can do some of your work.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: It's very quiet for you to concentrate.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: It sounds so special!

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: There are some other utilities I have to tell you.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: There is a garden in front of the house, in which you will find some interesting plants and beautiful flowers.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Do we have to take care of these plants and flowers?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Well, you don't have to worry about that.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: There will be a professional gardener to do the landscaping in the garden regularly.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: But you do need to help water the grass once a week.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Is it a problem for you?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Of course not.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: We'd like to do that.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Another thing is, in this district every household is arranged with a cleaner responsible for cleaning up the rubbish and recycling, but this service will cost you 15 dollars every month.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: What other bills do we have to pay?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Bills for electricity, water and gas will also be covered by yourselves.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: OK.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: One more question, is air-conditioning available in the house?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: If you mean central air-conditioning, I'm afraid there isn't at the moment, but each room has a conditioning unit which is installed on the window.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: That will be fine.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Anyway, I think it's a good place to live in.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Can we go and see the house, please?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Yes, of course.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: May I have your name and phone number?

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Yes, I'm Sam Dressler.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: That's S-A-M, D-R-E-double S-L-E-R.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: OK, Sam Dressler, I've got that down.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: And my phone number is 6753 4571.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Thank you very much.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: I'll contact you after discussing it with my two friends.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: No problem.

transcript.speaker: Landlord
transcript.en: Bye!

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.en: Bye!

1.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 date；提示词 date，此处应填日期。
题目解析：定位词 date 出现后，先听到了干扰信息 May 3rd，注意这是房东与现租客合同到期的日子（… is due），接着房东说需要花一天时间来打扫和整理，随后出现的 5th May 才是房子可租住的日期，其中 be ready 对应题干中的 Available。因此答案为 5(th) May 或 May 5(th)。

2.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 rent；提示词 $，此处应填数字。
题目解析：本题考查对数字的反应速度以及对干扰信息的辨识。定位词 rent 首先出现在学生说的话中，随后出现了好几个数字，不过需要注意这都是学生自己所说，分别为自己的理想价位（每人每月 500 美元）和最高可承受价位（每人每月 600 美元），都是干扰信息。注意听说话者的角色，答案会出现在房东的话中。在学生说完后，房东对 rent 进行说明，这里给出了 1700 这个数字，为真正的房租租金。因此答案为 1700。

3.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 $15；提示词 rent、deposit，此处应填名词，且与 rent 和 deposit 并列。
题目解析：本题答案前置，出现在定位词 $15 之前，如果死盯定位词可能会错过答案。通过之前的预判可知，本题答案应与 rent 和 deposit 并列，所以只要在 deposit 和 $1,500 出现后，有意识地听表示并列关系的逻辑衔接，然后抓取符合预判的词汇即可。本题可通过房东所说的 One important thing 这一表示强调的并列信号词锁定答案句：“… you'll be required to provide a credit check to guarantee your ability to pay the rent every month.”抓取其中符合预判的词汇即可，同时可通过之后出现的定位词 $15 进行确认。因此答案为 credit check。

4.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 remodelled，此处应填表示房间类型的名词。
题目解析：本题虽无好的定位词，但可通过上一个信息点 3 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms 来预判答案的出现，之后有意识地抓取表示房间类型的名词即可。答案句为：“Then there is a shared kitchen which was refurbished several months ago.”其中 refurbished 替换了题干 remodelled。因此答案为 kitchen。

5.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 no，此处应填名词。
题目解析：通过提示词 no 可知，本题的答案应该是被否定了的信息。通过学生对房东的回答“we like cooking”以及提问“Do we have to buy our own cookers?”可知，接下来讨论的内容有关厨房设备。答案信息由转折信号词 but 引出：“… but if you want a dishwasher, I'm sorry it needs to be added later.”其中 it needs to be added later 替换了题干中的 no。因此答案为 dishwasher。

6.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 work area；提示词 a，此处应填表示房间类型的名词。
题目解析：本题同样是答案前置，出现在定位词 work area 之前。建议通过学生的提问“Is there any place we can park?”去定位下一条信息的出现，房东随即给出答案：“Yes, there is a garage which not only provides space for your car but also has a special section where you can do some of your work.”抓取其中表示房间类型的词汇即可，再根据后面提到的 work area 的同义替换 a special section … do some of your work 进行确认。因此答案为 garage。

7.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 landscaping、grass；提示词 must，此处应填动词原形。
题目解析：本题难度不大，定位词 landscaping 和 grass 均不容易替换。landscaping 出现后，通过转折信号词 But 锁定答案句：“But you do need to help water the grass once a week.”其中 need to 替换了提示词 must，比较容易识别。因此答案为 water。

8.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 $15、trashing；提示词 and，此处应填与 trashing 并列的名词。
题目解析：本题定位词 $15 出现于答案之后，且 trashing 在录音中被替换为 cleaning up the rubbish。能识别出这一同义替换即可通过并列连词 and 找到答案词，然后再通过 $15 进行验证即可。当然如果无法一下子识别出 trashing 的同义替换，也可以通过对话的语义推进来定位，接第 7 题之后出现了并列信号词 Another thing is，从而可锁定答案句即将出现，重点听并列出现的信息即可。因此答案为 recycling。

9.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 central air conditioning、conditioning unit；提示词 a、conditioning unit，此处应填名词或形容词。
题目解析：首先听到定位词 central air conditioning 原词重现，随后答案信息由转折信号词 but 引出：“… but each room has a conditioning unit which is installed on the window.”其中另一定位词 conditioning unit 也原词重现，可以修饰它的词只有名词 window。因此答案为 window。

10.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Name；提示词 Name，此处应填人名。
题目解析：本题比较简单。雅思听力中考查人名时通常会给出拼写，所以注意跟上录音即可。本题的人名中有两个 s，读法为 double s。因此答案为 Dressler。
